title: Bridge Essay: Hyperscanning literature after two decades of neuroscientific research: A scientometric review.
concept: Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

**Hyperscanning literature after two decades of neuroscientific research: A scientometric review.** - Neuroscience (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroscience.2024.05.045. PMID: 38866073. Hyperscanning, a neuroimaging approach introduced in 2002 for simultaneously recording the brain activity of multiple participants, has significantly contributed to our understanding of social interactions. Nevertheless, the existing literature requires systematic organization to advance our knowledge. This study, after two decades of hyperscanning research, aims to identify the primary thematic domains and the most influential documents in the field. We conducted a scientometric analysis to exa
